title: A100 (Nvidia Ampere)
existing_short_definition: Nvidia's Ampere-architecture data-center GPU that powered most of the pre-H100 LLM era — GPT-3, early Llama, and most 2020–2022 research.

title: Accelerate (Hugging Face)
existing_short_definition: A lightweight HF library that abstracts distributed training backends (DDP, FSDP, DeepSpeed, Megatron) behind a small common API.

title: ALiBi (Attention with Linear Biases)
existing_short_definition: A position-encoding alternative that adds a linear bias to attention scores based on query-key distance, enabling strong extrapolation to unseen sequence lengths.

title: Anthropic Workbench / Console
existing_short_definition: Anthropic's web-based prompt development environment for building, testing, and deploying prompts with Claude — equivalent to OpenAI Playground.

title: AutoGen
existing_short_definition: Microsoft's multi-agent conversation framework where agents with distinct roles collaborate to solve tasks via structured dialogue.

title: Axolotl
existing_short_definition: A YAML-configured LLM fine-tuning framework covering SFT, LoRA, QLoRA, DPO, ORPO, full fine-tune, and multi-GPU training — the Swiss Army knife of LLM training.

title: B200 (Nvidia Blackwell)
existing_short_definition: Nvidia's Blackwell-architecture GPU (2024+), doubling H100 performance with FP4 support and larger HBM3e memory — the new frontier training platform.

title: BAML (Boundary AI Markup Language)
existing_short_definition: A domain-specific language for defining LLM functions with typed inputs/outputs, prompt templates, and test cases — compiled to Python or TypeScript.

title: BentoML
existing_short_definition: An open-source framework for packaging and serving ML models as REST/gRPC endpoints with Docker-ready deployment artifacts.

title: BitsAndBytes
existing_short_definition: A library for 8-bit and 4-bit quantization of LLMs at load time, enabling bigger models to fit on smaller GPUs via drop-in Transformers integration.

title: Chunked Prefill
existing_short_definition: An inference scheduling technique that splits long prefills into chunks so decoding tokens from other requests can interleave, reducing tail latency.

title: Sandbox (LLM Code Execution)
existing_short_definition: Isolated environments where LLM-generated code runs safely — typically Docker containers, Firecracker microVMs, or WebAssembly.

title: Cog (Replicate)
existing_short_definition: Replicate's open-source tool for packaging ML models into production-ready Docker containers with typed predict functions.

title: Context Length Extension
existing_short_definition: Post-training techniques that extend a model's usable context window beyond its training length, typically via RoPE scaling plus a short fine-tune.

title: Context Window
existing_short_definition: The context window is how much text an AI can read and remember at once — like its short-term memory.

title: Continuous Batching (Inflight Batching)
existing_short_definition: LLM serving optimization where new requests join an in-progress batch at each decoding step, instead of waiting for the batch to complete.

title: CoreML (Apple)
existing_short_definition: Apple's on-device ML framework for iOS, macOS, and visionOS — running models on Apple Neural Engine, GPU, or CPU depending on what fits best.

title: CPU Offloading
existing_short_definition: A memory-saving technique that stores some model weights or optimizer state in CPU RAM and streams them to the GPU as needed.

title: CrewAI
existing_short_definition: A Python framework for multi-agent orchestration where agents have roles, goals, and backstories, collaborating through defined processes.

title: CUDA
existing_short_definition: Nvidia's parallel-compute platform and programming model that nearly every ML framework targets, creating the moat around Nvidia GPUs.

title: Decode (Generation Phase)
existing_short_definition: The LLM inference phase where tokens are generated one at a time, each pass reusing the KV cache — memory-bandwidth bound, not compute-bound.

title: Diffusers (Library)
existing_short_definition: Hugging Face's library for diffusion models — Stable Diffusion, FLUX, Sora-style, SVD — with a consistent API across image, video, and audio generation.

title: Disk Offloading (NVMe Offload)
existing_short_definition: Extending offloading beyond CPU RAM to NVMe SSDs, enabling training of trillion-parameter models on modest clusters at major throughput cost.

title: Docker (ML Containers)
existing_short_definition: The standard container format for packaging ML code, models, and dependencies into reproducible, deployable images.

title: DSPy
existing_short_definition: Stanford's framework for programming (not prompting) LLMs — you write modules and signatures, DSPy compiles and optimizes the prompts automatically.

title: Inngest / Temporal / Dagster (Durable Workflows)
existing_short_definition: General-purpose durable execution engines increasingly used to orchestrate long-running, crash-resistant AI agent workflows.

title: Dynamic Batching
existing_short_definition: A batching strategy where requests are grouped on the fly based on a time window or max batch size rather than waiting for a pre-defined batch.

title: ExLlama (ExLlamaV2)
existing_short_definition: A fast GPU-only inference library for quantized Llama-family models, popular for consumer-GPU deployments due to superior speed on GPTQ/EXL2 models.

title: FastAPI (LLM Backends)
existing_short_definition: The dominant Python framework for building production LLM APIs — async-native, Pydantic-typed, OpenAPI-generated.

title: Fireworks AI
existing_short_definition: An inference provider focused on fast, low-latency open-source LLM serving with fine-tuning, structured output, and vision support.

title: Flash Attention
existing_short_definition: A GPU-aware attention algorithm that computes the exact same math as standard attention but ~2–4x faster and with dramatically less memory use.
existing_code_example:
# Enable Flash Attention in Transformers
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
    'meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B',
    attn_implementation='flash_attention_2',
    torch_dtype='bfloat16',
)

title: Flash Attention 2 / 3
existing_short_definition: Successive versions of Flash Attention that improve GPU occupancy, reduce non-matmul overhead, and add support for new precisions and hardware.

title: GGUF
existing_short_definition: A file format for AI models that's optimized for running on your own computer, including on CPU.

title: GH200 (Nvidia Grace Hopper)
existing_short_definition: Nvidia's coherent-memory superchip pairing a Grace CPU with an H100 GPU via 900 GB/s NVLink, useful for massive-context LLMs and large model inference.

title: GPU (Graphics Processing Unit)
existing_short_definition: Massively parallel processors with thousands of cores optimized for matrix math — the workhorse of nearly all ML training and inference.

title: Gradio
existing_short_definition: A Python library for building ML model demos and web UIs with minimal code — the backbone of most Hugging Face Spaces demos.

title: Groq (LPU)
existing_short_definition: Groq's custom Language Processing Units and their inference service, achieving extremely high tokens-per-second throughput (500+ tok/s) on popular LLMs.

title: GQA (Grouped Query Attention)
existing_short_definition: An attention variant where multiple query heads share the same key/value heads, reducing KV cache size and memory bandwidth without much quality loss.

title: H100 (Nvidia Hopper)
existing_short_definition: Nvidia's Hopper-architecture data-center GPU — the workhorse of frontier LLM training and inference from 2023–2024.

title: HBM (High Bandwidth Memory)
existing_short_definition: Stacked DRAM integrated with a GPU or accelerator via a silicon interposer, delivering much higher bandwidth than GDDR memory.

title: Hugging Face Datasets
existing_short_definition: A library for loading, processing, and streaming ML datasets with memory-mapped access and deep Hub integration.

title: Hugging Face Hub
existing_short_definition: The GitHub-of-ML-models — hosts 1M+ model, dataset, and space repositories with git-LFS versioning, model cards, and a unified loading API.

title: Hugging Face Transformers
existing_short_definition: The dominant library for loading, running, and fine-tuning pretrained transformer models — BERT, Llama, Stable Diffusion, Whisper, everything.

title: Inference as a Service
existing_short_definition: Managed inference endpoints from providers (Together, Fireworks, Groq, Replicate, Anyscale) that host open-source LLMs behind an OpenAI-compatible API.

title: InfiniBand
existing_short_definition: A high-performance networking standard used between servers in GPU clusters to connect nodes with low-latency, high-bandwidth RDMA.

title: Instructor (Pydantic for LLMs)
existing_short_definition: A Python library that makes LLMs return Pydantic-validated structured outputs with automatic retries and schema-based prompting.

title: JAX
existing_short_definition: Google's array framework combining NumPy-like API with JIT compilation (XLA), automatic differentiation, and pmap/pjit for scaling to TPU pods.

title: Kubernetes (ML Workloads)
existing_short_definition: The default container orchestration platform for production ML — GPU scheduling, autoscaling, job lifecycle, and multi-tenant isolation.

title: KV Cache
existing_short_definition: A memory buffer that stores attention keys and values from previous tokens so the model doesn't recompute them on every new token — the #1 reason LLM inference is feasible.
existing_code_example:
# Estimate KV cache size for a model
# KV cache (bytes) = 2 * n_layers * n_heads * head_dim * seq_len * dtype_bytes * batch_size
# Llama-3-70B @ 32k context, bf16, batch 1:
# = 2 * 80 * 8 * 128 * 32_000 * 2 * 1 = 10.5 GB
def kv_cache_bytes(layers, heads, head_dim, seq, dtype_bytes=2, batch=1):
    return 2 * layers * heads * head_dim * seq * dtype_bytes * batch

title: L4 / L40S (Inference GPUs)
existing_short_definition: Nvidia's inference-optimized Ada Lovelace GPUs — L4 (24GB, edge/dev) and L40S (48GB, server) — lower TDP alternatives to H100 for serving.

title: LangChain
existing_short_definition: A Python/JS framework for building LLM applications — chains, agents, retrievers, memory, and 700+ integrations with models, DBs, and tools.

title: LangGraph
existing_short_definition: LangChain's graph-based orchestration library for building stateful, multi-agent LLM workflows with explicit node/edge semantics and durable execution.

title: Liger Kernels
existing_short_definition: LinkedIn's open-source Triton-based kernels for LLM training that deliver significant speedups and memory savings over stock PyTorch implementations.

title: llama.cpp (server)
existing_short_definition: The inference server built into the llama.cpp project — a lightweight C/C++ HTTP server that runs GGUF models with minimal dependencies.

title: LlamaIndex
existing_short_definition: A framework optimized for RAG — indexing, retrieval, query engines, and agent workflows with a focus on connecting LLMs to private data.

title: LM Studio
existing_short_definition: A cross-platform desktop app for running LLMs locally with a GUI and OpenAI-compatible local server — no command line needed.

title: Lost in the Middle
existing_short_definition: A long-context failure pattern where models use information at the beginning and end of context well but ignore content in the middle.

title: Mem0
existing_short_definition: An open-source memory layer for AI applications that stores, retrieves, and updates user- and session-level memories across any LLM.

title: MemGPT (Letta)
existing_short_definition: A memory architecture (now called Letta) that gives LLMs OS-like memory management — paging context in and out of limited working memory.

title: MI300X (AMD Instinct)
existing_short_definition: AMD's data-center GPU with 192GB HBM3 and strong bf16 performance — a credible non-Nvidia option for LLM inference and some training.

title: MLC-LLM
existing_short_definition: A universal LLM deployment framework that compiles models for any device — iOS, Android, WebGPU, CUDA, Metal, Vulkan — from a single codebase.

title: MLX (Apple)
existing_short_definition: Apple's array framework for Apple Silicon — a PyTorch-like API with lazy evaluation optimized for unified memory architecture on M-series chips.

title: Modal (Serverless GPU)
existing_short_definition: A serverless GPU platform where you write Python functions with decorators and Modal handles containers, GPUs, scaling, and cold starts.

title: MQA (Multi-Query Attention)
existing_short_definition: An attention variant where all query heads share a single key/value head pair, maximally reducing KV cache size.

title: NCCL (Nvidia Collective Communications Library)
existing_short_definition: Nvidia's optimized library for multi-GPU and multi-node collective operations — all-reduce, all-gather, broadcast — the backbone of distributed training.

title: NVLink
existing_short_definition: Nvidia's high-bandwidth, low-latency GPU-to-GPU and CPU-to-GPU interconnect, enabling efficient multi-GPU training and inference.

title: Ollama
existing_short_definition: A developer-friendly runtime for running LLMs locally via simple CLI/REST commands, wrapping llama.cpp with a curated model library.

title: ONNX (Open Neural Network Exchange)
existing_short_definition: An open format for representing ML models so they can move between frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX) and runtimes (ONNX Runtime, TensorRT).

title: ONNX Runtime
existing_short_definition: Microsoft's cross-platform ML inference engine that runs ONNX models efficiently on CPUs, GPUs, mobile devices, and browsers.

title: OpenVINO (Intel)
existing_short_definition: Intel's inference toolkit for deploying ML models on Intel CPUs, iGPUs, dGPUs, and specialized accelerators — strong on CPU inference optimization.

title: PagedAttention
existing_short_definition: A KV cache management technique from vLLM that stores cache in non-contiguous fixed-size blocks (pages), allowing memory sharing and eliminating fragmentation.

title: PEFT Library (HF)
existing_short_definition: Hugging Face's library implementing LoRA, QLoRA, DoRA, adapters, prompt tuning, IA3, and most major PEFT methods behind a unified API.

title: Prefill
existing_short_definition: The first phase of LLM inference where the entire prompt is processed in parallel to initialize the KV cache — compute-bound and fast on GPUs.

title: Prefix Caching (Inference)
existing_short_definition: Reusing the KV cache of shared prompt prefixes across requests to skip recomputation — the inference-engine counterpart to API-level prompt caching.

title: Pydantic
existing_short_definition: Python's most popular data-validation library — models with typed fields that parse, validate, and serialize data, heavily used for LLM inputs/outputs.

title: Pydantic AI
existing_short_definition: The Pydantic team's framework for building LLM agents with the same ergonomics as Pydantic models — typed inputs, validated outputs, dependency injection.

title: PyTorch
existing_short_definition: The most widely used deep-learning framework — eager-mode Python APIs with autograd, GPU acceleration, and a vast ecosystem of libraries and models.

title: RadixAttention
existing_short_definition: SGLang's KV cache data structure — a radix tree that automatically discovers and reuses shared prompt prefixes across concurrent requests.

title: Ray (Distributed Python)
existing_short_definition: Anyscale's distributed Python framework — actors, tasks, and Ray Serve/Train/Tune — used for scaling ML workloads across clusters.

title: Ray Serve
existing_short_definition: Ray's model-serving library with native Python APIs, dynamic batching, autoscaling, and deployment graph composition.

title: Replicate
existing_short_definition: A model-hosting platform where community-contributed models (LLMs, image gen, video gen, audio) are packaged as 'Cog' containers and served on-demand.

title: RMSNorm
existing_short_definition: Root Mean Square Normalization — a normalization layer that skips the mean-centering of LayerNorm, saving compute with equivalent quality.

title: ROCm (AMD)
existing_short_definition: AMD's open-source alternative to CUDA — compute platform, runtime, and libraries for running ML workloads on AMD Instinct GPUs.

title: RoPE (Rotary Position Embedding)
existing_short_definition: A position encoding that rotates query and key vectors by position-dependent angles, enabling relative position awareness and straightforward context-length extension.

title: RoPE Scaling / YaRN
existing_short_definition: Techniques for extending a RoPE-based model's context window beyond its training length by reshaping the rotation frequencies.

title: RunPod
existing_short_definition: A GPU cloud platform offering on-demand and spot instances at discount prices, with serverless endpoints for inference workloads.

title: Safetensors
existing_short_definition: A secure file format for storing model weights — fast, memory-mappable, and immune to the arbitrary code execution vulnerabilities in pickle-based formats.

title: SGLang
existing_short_definition: A fast LLM serving engine with a programming frontend for complex prompt workflows and RadixAttention for KV cache reuse across prompt prefixes.

title: Sliding Window Attention
existing_short_definition: An attention pattern where each token attends only to the last K tokens rather than the full sequence — linear-cost attention at the price of limited long-range context.

title: Streamlit
existing_short_definition: A Python library for building data apps and dashboards with pure Python — a common choice for internal LLM tools and prototypes.

title: SwiGLU Activation
existing_short_definition: A gated linear unit activation that combines Swish and GLU, used in most modern LLM feed-forward layers for better quality than plain ReLU/GELU.

title: TensorRT-LLM
existing_short_definition: Nvidia's optimized LLM inference library that compiles model graphs to TensorRT with custom kernels for maximum H100/B200 throughput.

title: Text Generation Inference (TGI)
existing_short_definition: Hugging Face's LLM inference server — production-ready, supports most models, integrates with the HF ecosystem.

title: Together AI
existing_short_definition: A major inference-as-a-service provider hosting a wide catalog of open-source LLMs with competitive per-token pricing and fine-tuning endpoints.

title: torch.compile
existing_short_definition: PyTorch 2.0's JIT compiler that traces Python code into a graph and compiles it via Inductor (Triton backend) for significant speedups.

title: TPU (Tensor Processing Unit)
existing_short_definition: Google's custom ML accelerator — TPU v4, v5, v6 — optimized for massive matmul throughput via systolic arrays, used to train Gemini and Gemma.

title: AWS Trainium / Inferentia
existing_short_definition: Amazon's custom training (Trainium) and inference (Inferentia) accelerators, accessed via AWS Neuron SDK as a lower-cost alternative to Nvidia on AWS.

title: Transformers.js
existing_short_definition: Hugging Face's JavaScript port of Transformers, letting models run in Node.js or the browser via ONNX Runtime Web (CPU) or WebGPU (GPU).

title: Triton (Language / Compiler)
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's open-source Python-like DSL for writing GPU kernels that often match hand-tuned CUDA performance with far less code.

title: Triton Inference Server (Nvidia)
existing_short_definition: Nvidia's open-source inference serving framework supporting multiple backends (TensorRT, PyTorch, ONNX, Python, TensorRT-LLM) with HTTP/gRPC endpoints.

title: TRL (Transformers Reinforcement Learning)
existing_short_definition: Hugging Face's library for RLHF and preference optimization — SFTTrainer, DPOTrainer, PPOTrainer, KTOTrainer, RewardTrainer.

title: Unsloth
existing_short_definition: An open-source LLM fine-tuning library that delivers 2x training speed and 80% memory savings on consumer GPUs through custom Triton kernels and memory optimizations.

title: Vector Database
existing_short_definition: A vector database stores embeddings and lets you search for similar ones extremely fast.

title: vLLM
existing_short_definition: A high-throughput LLM inference and serving engine from UC Berkeley, centered on PagedAttention for efficient KV cache management.

title: vLLM OpenAI API Server
existing_short_definition: vLLM's built-in OpenAI-compatible REST server that lets any OpenAI SDK client talk to local vLLM-served models.

title: VRAM
existing_short_definition: Your GPU's dedicated memory — the key bottleneck for running local AI models.

title: WebGPU
existing_short_definition: A modern browser graphics/compute API (successor to WebGL) that enables GPU-accelerated ML inference in the browser without plugins.
